revision_summary: One critical fabrication requires correction and cascading fixes: Stage 3 misattributes 'Nah. I feel you there.' (spoken by Justin, speaker_1, at 01:11) to Meg. This inverts the empathy coaching read entirely — Meg offered zero empathy acknowledgment before pivoting to pitch, which is a harder miss than Stage 3 describes. All downstream reasoning in cherry_pick_moments[2] and cherry_pick_moments[3] must be corrected: (1) Remove the claim that Meg said 'I feel you there.' (2) Rewrite the empathy_miss entry at 01:09/01:14 to reflect that Meg's actual response to Justin's financial disclosure was an immediate pitch pivot with no empathy beat at all. (3) Reclassify cherry_pick_moments[3] (01:21) from knowledge_gap/bill_anchoring to empathy_on_vulnerability_disclosure — no bill was ever discussed on this call, making the bill_anchoring codex reference inapplicable. Additionally: (4) Remove the fabricated '40 seconds' duration claim in cherry_pick_moments[4] — the sun-study explanation spans approximately 24 seconds per the transcript; replace with 'roughly 20-25 seconds' or remove the specific duration. (5) Add a missed moment at 00:58 ('Well, have you ever looked at solar?') as the root-cause moment where Meg skips the verify section and pivots to interest-probing — this gives the homeownership-miss coaching at 02:44 a cleaner causal chain. (6) Flip the alternative_hypothesis resolution at 00:52/00:53: the household-member probe is better read as an indirect legitimacy attempt (the alternative hypothesis Stage 3 itself raised) than as pure reactive deflection — the primary and alternative reads should be swapped with supporting reasoning.
